texto: The thing about families, Arlo thought, was that there was always some question nobody wanted to answer for you, and it was like a stray thread pulling loose in a sweater. You could tug at it all you wanted, but in the end, all you'd have was a pile of twisted yarn.
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a Sarah Sullivan: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de temas ligados a familia. O trecho “The thing about families, Arlo thought, was that there was always some question nobody wanted to …” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que familia, families, family-relationships pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de termino e family-relationships.

frase_original: The thing about families, Arlo thought, was that there was always some question nobody wanted to answer for you, and it was like a stray thread pulling loose in a sweater. You could tug at it all you wanted, but in the end, all you'd have was a pile of twisted yarn.
